exemplar.title:
To what extent is sand dune formation in Mambrui, Kilifi County, influenced by wind processes ,

soil acidity, and vegetation cover?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent is sand dune formation in Mambrui, Kilifi County, influenced by wind processes ,

soil acidity, and vegetation cover?

subject.title: Geography
subject.description:
**IB Geography** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **physical and human geography, environmental interactions, global challenges, and spatial patterns**, with a strong focus on **fieldwork, data analysis, case studies, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS)**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Geographic themes - structured questions | 2h 15min | 2h 15min | SL: 35% / HL: 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Geographic perspectives - data response and essays | 1h 15min | 1h 15min | SL: 40% / HL: 45% |
| **Paper 3** | Global interactions - extended essay responses | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Fieldwork investigation (2,500 words) | 20 hours | 20 hours | SL: 25% / HL: 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly geographical with explicit theoretical underpinning and testable hypotheses.
Precise location (Mambrui dunes) with coordinates, distance/bearing and demonstration of feasibility for primary data collection.
Hypotheses are comprehensive, reflecting a strong link to Option B coastal margin theory.
weaknesses: Locational map lacks cartographic conventions (scale bar, north arrow, legend).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands reached the top band (question formulation and geographic context) with sharp theoretical links and precise site description, while the mapping strand showed only partial adherence to conventions. Overall performance merits the maximum 3 marks.
feedback.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

strengths: Methods are described clearly with equipment specified (anemometer, pH meter, quadrat) and demonstrate replicability.
Justification for sampling frame and equipment choice is provided, and field photographs illustrate correct technique.
weaknesses: Sampling distances list contains duplicates/omissions, indicating procedural inconsistency.
Theoretical alignment to dune-process literature is only briefly asserted, limiting the depth of method justification.
Data set remains modest, reducing reliability of findings.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Methods are relevant and described with reasonable clarity, but procedural inconsistencies and limited reliability place the work solidly in the mid band (2 marks).

strengths: Core data collected (wind speed, pH, vegetation cover) are directly relevant to the hypotheses.
Graphs (scatter, bar charts) and a Spearman calculation are appropriate and generally wellâlabelled.
Figures are numbered and titled with units on axes, demonstrating mostly correct conventions.
weaknesses: Data set is sparse (no sediment size measurements), limiting completeness.
Occasional errors in labelling/numbering (e.g., inconsistent figure numbering).
Formatting errors in statistical formula presentation and citation inconsistencies.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Most data are relevant and sufficient for meaningful analysis; presentation methods are appropriate, and conventions are generally adhered to. Occasional lapses prevent a topâband score, so 4 marks accurately reflect quality.
feedback.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

strengths: Both descriptive statistics and a Spearman rank test are applied, demonstrating integration of graphical and statistical analysis.
Trends and anomalies (e.g., pHâvegetation link, wind anomalies) are identified and linked back to the research question.
All three hypotheses are revisited in the analysis, showing logical coherence.
weaknesses: The Spearman coefficient is miscomputed and formula layout is unclear.
Theoretical interpretation (aeolian transport mechanisms) remains thin.
Gaps in data (intermediate dune heights, sediment sizes) limit the completeness of analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis goes beyond basic description by employing statistical techniques and connecting findings to the research question. Some miscalculations and gaps remain, but overall the work demonstrates comprehensive analytical effort worthy of 5 marks.

strengths: Conclusion clearly restates key findings for each variable with specific data values.
Statements are directly supported by the preceding analysis, meeting topâband requirements.
weaknesses: The conclusion is concise but could be expanded by discussing broader geographic implications or limitations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is clear, directly supported by evidence, and aligns fully with the analysis, warranting the maximum 2 marks for this criterion.

strengths: Evaluation identifies multiple strengths and weaknesses and explains their impact on validity (e.g., spatial scope, seasonal limitation).
Improvement suggestions (extending transect coverage, longâterm monitoring) are detailed with a clear rationale for how they would enhance results.
Reflections on sampling strategy demonstrate critical awareness.
weaknesses: Inconsistency noted between claimed random sampling and described systematic approach.
Could provide more detail on how changes in design would quantitatively affect findings.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation provides a comprehensive explanation of methodological strengths/weaknesses and detailed, impact-focused improvement suggestions, meeting the highest band for 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: A location map is provided but is hand-drawn, lacks a scale bar and north arrow, and key symbols are text boxes rather than conventional cartographic symbols. While it helps the reader find sites, mapping conventions are only partially met, fitting the mid-band description.

The research question is clearly geographical, focuses on aeolian processes and biotic factors, is sharply phrased ("to what extent â¦") and directly underpinned by option B theory on coastal margins. Three explicit hypotheses flow from it, demonstrating scope for testing. This aligns with the top-band descriptor.

Scatter graphs, bar charts and a Spearman calculation are appropriate and clearly labelled. More sophisticated coastal diagrams or GIS mapping are absent, so presentation is effective but not the âmost effectiveâ, situating the work in the middle of the 3-4 band.

Techniques (anemometer, pH meter, quadrat) were appropriate and repeated trials undertaken, yet the data set remains modest (nine transect points; limited vegetation/dune-height readings) and some procedural inconsistencies appear in the distance list. Reliability is reasonable but not high-quality.

Descriptive statistics and a Spearman rank test are attempted, demonstrating use of both graphical and statistical tools. The coefficient is mis-computed (sign error), indicating partial accuracy; therefore the work meets but does not exceed the 3-4 band.

The student links falling pH to vegetation stability and discusses wind anomalies; trends and some anomalies are connected to hypotheses, yet theoretical depth (aeolian transport mechanics) is thin. This fits the 3-4 descriptor.

The student specifies Mambrui dunes with full coordinates, distance/bearing from nearby settlements and highlights feasibility for on-site primary data. The context paragraph situates the dunes within Kenyaâs coastal system, satisfying âprecise location with clear feasibilityâ.

Strengths and weaknesses are identified (spatial scope, seasonal limitation, quadrat sampling) with brief explanations of why they matter, fulfilling an âappropriate outlineâ rather than a comprehensive evaluation.

Core data (wind speed, pH, vegetation, limited dune height) are relevant to all three hypotheses, but the vegetation/dune sample is sparse and no sediment-size data were collected. Thus most, not all, data are sufficientâmatching the 3-4 band; a score of 3 reflects the evident shortfalls.

The conclusion restates key findings for each variable and cites specific data (e.g., 9.21 m/s, pH trends), showing direct support from analysis. Although brief, it is clear and fully aligned, warranting the top band for this criterion.

All three hypotheses are revisited, but vegetation analysis is restricted and no sediment size statistics are provided, leaving moderate gaps. Completeness is therefore âanalysis with some gapsâ, the middle band.

Methods for wind, pH, vegetation and distance are described with equipment, sampling frame and justification; however, theoretical alignment to dune process literature is only briefly asserted and some distance intervals are duplicated/omitted, so the explanation is clear but not fully comprehensive.

Figures are numbered and titled; axes include units; however, several spelling errors ("shreline", "Indian ocean") and an incorrect Spearman formula layout show occasional lapses. This equates to âoccasional errors in conventionsâ (3-4 band, lower end).

Suggestionsâextend spatial coverage, adopt long-term monitoringâare relevant but lack detail on how they would alter results. The response therefore fits ârelevant improvement suggestions outlinedâ, awarding the mid-band score.

annotations.comment: The vegetation vs. dune height scatter lacks a regression equation and RÂ²; include these to quantify the strength of the relationship.
The research question is clearly geographical and formulated sharply with scope for hypothesis testing, reflecting a high-level theoretical link.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The table of contents shows duplicate numbering for section 2.1, which may confuse the reader and hinder navigation.
Coordinate notation should follow conventional format (e.g., degrees, minutes, seconds) rather than â3Â°0714.8 Sâ to improve precision and clarity.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The locational map lacks essential cartographic conventions such as a scale bar, north arrow and legend, reducing its clarity and usefulness.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The interpretation of Hâ in the analysis contradicts the original hypothesis direction regarding acidic vs. alkaline soil stability. Please reconcile these.
Hypothesis Hâ on vegetation and dune stability is clearly stated, demonstrating a comprehensive research focus.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The link to syllabus demonstrates relevance, but could be deepened by referencing specific coastal margin models or key theorists.
annotations.groupName: Criterion A: Fieldwork Question and Geographic Context

annotations.comment: The list of sampling distances contains duplicates and omissions, indicating procedural inconsistencies in transect setup.
Detailed description of the tape-measure method ensures replicability and demonstrates strong procedural rigor.
Using a single quadrat per point limits vegetation data reliability. Add multiple replicates at each site to strengthen findings.
Caption refers to âFig. 3.0â but the figure is numbered inconsistently as Figure 2, causing confusion in data presentation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Text describes pH âdecreaseâ with distance, contradicting earlier statement of a positive correlation; clarify the trend analysis.
The photograph of the anemometer placement at 2 m height is excellent and effectively demonstrates correct field technique.
The subsection heading for measuring distance appears before its description, disrupting flow. Consider relocating it after the introductory text.
The percentage vegetation cover formula is correctly presented and follows standard notation.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: You mention measuring dune heights and widths in the justification but provide no methodological details; include a clear procedure for these measurements.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Gaps in intermediate dune heights limit interpretation; discuss collection of data points between 40 cm and 70 cm.
The interpretation of Hâ in this section shifts from acidic stability to alkaline stability; ensure consistency between hypothesis and analysis.
The description of windspeed trend under Hâ conflicts with the later Spearman result; please reconcile these differing findings.
Attributing wind anomalies solely to tidal action is speculative; include literature or data to substantiate this causal link.
The Spearman correlation table is well structured, but the formula layout in the caption is confusing; use clear parentheses and line breaks.
In the equation block, the line âr=1-(6x222)/729-9â is misformatted; ensure the denominator is clearly shown as \(n^3-n\).
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information

annotations.comment: Reinterpretation of the Spearman result again contradicts earlier discussion of windspeed distance trends; maintain analytical consistency.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes key findings with specific data, demonstrating full support from the analysis.
The evaluation outlines spatial limitations but could explain how expanding the transect would alter the results.
Strength item claims random sampling but methodology describes systematic sampling, indicating an inconsistency in sampling strategy.
Several references lack consistent formatting and complete information; apply a uniform citation style (e.g., APA) across all entries.
annotations.groupName: Criterion C: Quality and Treatment of Information
